purpose: An icon-only button that opens a Menu when clicked. Space-efficient way to expose a menu of actions when a label would take too much space (toolbars, table row actions, dense list items).
whenToUse: Row actions in a Table where each row has 3+ actions
Overflow menus in toolbars when horizontal space is tight
Card header 'More' menu (typically a three-dots icon)
whenNotToUse: For a single action — use IconButton
For 6+ actions — use a proper Menu with labels
When the icon meaning is unclear without a label — use MenuButton
For primary actions — use Button with a clear label
doRules: Always provide a tooltip and aria-label describing what the menu contains
Use standardized icons: three dots (⋮) for 'more', gear for settings, filter icon for filter menu
Show the chevron only when space allows (or omit for kebab-style)
Anchor the menu below the button by default
dontRules: Do not omit the tooltip — icon-only is fine, but the icon must be discoverable
Do not use ambiguous icons — pick well-known ones or fall back to labeled MenuButton
Do not use for a single action — IconButton is correct there
Do not stack multiple IconMenuButtons in the same row unless separated visually

layoutGuidance.placement: Toolbars, table row action columns, card headers
layoutGuidance.alignment: Right-aligned in row actions; left-aligned in toolbars

contentGuidance.contentRules: Icon must be well-known or heavily used across the app

requirements: aria-label describing the menu contents ('Row actions')
aria-haspopup='menu' and aria-expanded reflect menu state
Keyboard: Enter opens; Down opens with focus in menu; Escape closes
